name: TShark (Wireshark CLI Network Analyzer)

performance.notes: Wire-speed capture; file analysis speed depends on pcap size

agent_readiness.notes: CLI Wireshark for packet capture and analysis. tshark -i eth0 (live capture). tshark -r capture.pcap (analyze file). Display filters: tshark -Y 'http.request' -r file.pcap. -T fields -e http.host -e ip.src (extract fields). JSON/PDML/CSV output: -T json. BPF capture filters: -f 'tcp port 80'. --export-objects http,/tmp/out (extract HTTP objects). Scripting via pyshark Python wrapper. Follow streams: -z follow,tcp,ascii,0. Statistics: -z io,stat,1. Standard for CTF pcap challenges and network forensics.

gotchas: Large pcap files can be slow to analyze
Display filter syntax different from BPF capture filter syntax
Decryption requires SSL keys or decryption configured
best_when: Network traffic analysis and protocol debugging
Scripted packet extraction (field extraction to CSV/JSON)
avoid_when: Unauthorized packet capture (illegal on networks you don't own)
summary: TShark is the CLI equivalent of Wireshark — the standard packet capture and analysis tool. Live capture or pcap file analysis. Display filters, field extraction to JSON/CSV, BPF capture filters, HTTP object extraction. pyshark wraps it for Python automation. CTF pcap challenge staple. Network forensics essential. Requires root for live capture.
